name: CHEMISTRY (Theory)
question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 56/4/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A : Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each.
  3. Section B : Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each.
  4. Section C : Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each.
  5. Section D : Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
  7. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A
1.

Name the defect in the following crystal:

 1
2.

When a coordination compound CrCl3 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 6H2O is mixed with AgNO3, two moles of AgCl are precipitated per mole of the compound. What is the structural formula of the coordination compound?

 1

OR

What is the difference between a complex and a double salt?

 1
3.

Define associated colloid with an example.

 1
4.

Why is t-butyl bromide more reactive towards SN1 reaction as compared to n-butyl bromide?

 1
5.

Write the reaction involved in the Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction.

 1

OR

Propanamine and N,N-dimethylmethanamine contain the same number of carbon atoms, even though Propanamine has higher boiling point than N,N-dimethylmethanamine. Why?

 1

SECTION B
6.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. Aquatic species are more comfortable in cold water than warm water.
  2. At higher altitudes people suffer from anoxia resulting in inability to think.
 2

OR

What type of azeotropic mixture will be formed by a solution of acetone and chloroform? Justify on the basis of strength of intermolecular interactions that develop in the solution.

 2
7.

Explain with a graph, the variation of molar conductivity of a strong electrolyte with dilution.

 2
8.

When dilute ferrous sulphate solution is added to an aqueous solution containing nitrate ion followed by careful addition of concentrated sulphuric acid along the sides of test tube, a brown ring is formed at the interface between the solution and sulphuric acid layers. Which anion is confirmed by the appearance of brown ring? What is the composition of the brown ring?

 2

OR

How can you prepare Cl2 from HCl and HCl from Cl2 ? Write reactions only.

 2
9.

Use the data to answer the following and also justify giving reason:

  Cr Mn Fe Co
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{M}^{2+} / \mathrm{M}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} -0.91 -1.18 -0.44 -0.28
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{M}^{3+} / \mathrm{M}^{2+}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} -0.41 +1.57 +0.77 +1.97
  1. Which is a stronger reducing agent in aqueous medium, Cr2+ or Fe2+ and why?
  2. Which is the most stable ion in +2 oxidation and why?
 2
10.

Define with equation:

  1. Reimer-Tiemann reaction
  2. Williamson's Synthesis
 2
11.

Write the names and structures of monomers in the following polymers:

  1. Nylon-6,6
  2. Buna-S
 2
12.

Classify the following as addition and condensation polymers giving reason:

  1. Teflon
  2. PHBV
 2

SECTION C
13.

Chromium crystallises in bcc structure. If its edge length is 300 pm, find its density. Atomic mass of chromium is 52 u. [NA = 6.022 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 mol-1]

 3
14.

At 300 K, 30 g of glucose present in a litre of its solution has an osmotic pressure of 4.98 bar. If the osmotic pressure of a glucose solution is 1.52 bar at the same temperature, what would be its concentration?

 3
15.

Calculate {tex}\Delta_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{G}^{\circ}{/tex} and log Kc for the following reaction:
Cd+2(aq) + Zn(s) {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Zn2+(aq) + Cd(s)
Given: {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cd}}^{\mathrm{o}} 2+/ \mathrm{_C}_{d}{/tex} = -0.403 V
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}}^{\mathrm{O}} 2+/ \mathrm{_Z}{_n}{/tex} = -0.763 V

 3

OR

Chromium metal is electroplated using an acidic solution containing CrO3 according to the following equation:
CrO3(aq) + 6H+ + 6e- {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Cr(s) + 3H2O
Calculate how many grams of chromium will be electroplated by 24,000 coulombs. How long will it take to electroplate 1.5 g chromium using 12.5 A current?
[Atomic mass of Cr = 52 g mol-1, 1 F = 96500 C mol-1]

 3
16.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. Leather gets hardened after tanning.
  2. FeCl3 is preferred over KCl in case of a cut leading to bleeding.
  3. Freundlich isotherm becomes independent of pressure at high pressure for a gas absorbed on a solid.
 3
17.

What is the role of

  1. Depressants in froth floatation?
  2. Carbon monoxide in Mond’s process?
  3. Concentrated sodium hydroxide in leaching of alumina from bauxite?
 3

OR

Write chemical reactions taking place in the extraction of Aluminium from Bauxite ore.

 3
18.

Explain the method of preparation of sodium dichromate from chromite ore. Give the equation representing oxidation of ferrous salts by dichromate ion.

 3

OR

Complete the following reactions:

  1. MnO2 + KOH + O2 {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
  2. I- + {tex}\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}{/tex} + H+ {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
  3. {tex}\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}{/tex} + Sn2+ + H+ {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
 3
19.

Write the hybridization and magnetic character of the following complexes:

  1. [Fe(H2O)6]2+
  2. [Ni(CN)4]2-

[Atomic number: Fe = 26, Ni = 28]

 3
20.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. The presence of -NO2 group at ortho or para position increases the reactivity of haloarenes towards nucleophilic substitution reactions.
  2. p-dicholorobenzene has higher melting point than that of ortho or meta isomer.
  3. Thionyl chloride method is preferred for preparing alkyl chloride from alcohols.
 3

OR
  1. Write equation for preparation of 1-iodobutane from 1-chlorobutane.
  2. Out of 2-bromopentane, 2-bromo-2-methylbutane and 1-bromopentane, which compound is most reactive towards elimination reaction and why?
  3. Give IUPAC name of
 3
21.
  1. How will you synthesise the following alcohol from appropriate alkene:
  2. Write the mechanism of the following reaction:
    CH3CH2OH {tex}\xrightarrow[443 \mathrm{~K}]{\mathrm{H}^{+}}{/tex} CH2 = CH2 + H2O
 3
22.
  1. Give one chemical test to distinguish between the compounds of the following pairs:
    1. CH3NH2 and (CH3)2NH
    2. Aniline and Ethanamine
  2. Why aniline does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction?
 3
23.
  1. Give any one property of glucose that cannot be explained by the open chain structure.
  2. Compare amylase with amylopectin in terms of constituting structure.
  3. Why do amino acids show amphoteric behaviour?
 3
24.

Define the following with suitable example of each:

  1. Antiseptics
  2. Non-narcotic analgesics
  3. Cationic detergents
 3

SECTION D
25.
  1. Consider the reaction R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} P for which the change in concentration of R with time is shown by the following graph:
    1. Predict the order of reaction.
    2. What does the slope of the curve indicate?
  2. The rate of reaction quadruples when temperature changes from 293 K to 313 K. Calculate Ea assuming that it does not change with time. [R = 8.314 JK-1 mol-1]
 5

OR
  1. Draw the plot of ln k vs 1/T for a chemical reaction. What does the intercept represent? What is the relation between slope and Ea?
  2. A first order reaction takes 30 minutes for 20% decomposition. Calculate t1/2. [log 2 = 0.3010]
 5
26.
  1. Draw the structure of the following:
    1. HClO3
    2. H2S2O8
  2. Give reasons for the following:
    1. Above 1000 K sulphur shows paramagnetism.
    2. Although electron gain enthalpy of fluorine is less negative than that of chlorine, yet flourine is a better oxidising agent than chlorine.
    3. In solid state PCl5 exists as an ionic compound.
 5

OR
  1. Complete the following reactions:
    1. PbS(s) + O3 {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
    2. XeF6 + NaF {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
  2. Arrange the following in increasing order of property indicated, giving reason:
    1. Hydrides of group 15 - boiling points
    2. Hydrides of group 17 - acidic strength 
    3. Hydrides of group 16 - reducing character
 5
27.
  1. Carry out the following conversions:
    1. P-nitrotoluene to 2-bromobenzoic acid
    2. Propanoic acid to acetic acid
  2. An alkene with molecular formula C5H10 on ozonolysis gives a mixture of two compounds, B and C. Compound B gives positive Fehling test and also reacts with iodine and NaOH solution. Compound C does not give Fehling solution test but forms iodoform. Identify the compounds A, B and C.
 5

OR
  1. Carry out the following conversions:
    1. Benzoic acid to aniline
    2. Bromomethane to ethanol
  2. Write the structure of major product(s) in the following:
5

answer_key_html:
Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 56/4/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A : Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each.
  3. Section B : Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each.
  4. Section C : Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each.
  5. Section D : Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
  7. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A
1.

Name the defect in the following crystal:

Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 1
2.

When a coordination compound CrCl3 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 6H2O is mixed with AgNO3, two moles of AgCl are precipitated per mole of the compound. What is the structural formula of the coordination compound?

Solution:

[Cr (H2O)5Cl]Cl2{tex}\cdot{/tex} H2O / [Cr (H2O)5Cl]Cl2

 1

OR

What is the difference between a complex and a double salt?

Solution:

Double salt dissociates in simple ions completely when dissolved in water while Complex salt does not.

 1
3.

Define associated colloid with an example.

Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 1
4.

Why is t-butyl bromide more reactive towards SN1 reaction as compared to n-butyl bromide?

Solution:

Due to higher stability of 3o / tertiary carbocation

 1
5.

Write the reaction involved in the Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction.

Solution:

{tex}\mathrm R-\overset{\overset{\mathrm O}{\vert\vert}}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{NH}}_2+{\mathrm{Br}}_2+4\mathrm{NaOH}\rightarrow R-{\mathrm{NH}}_2+{\mathrm{Na}}_2{\mathrm{CO}}_3+2\mathrm{NaBr}+2{\mathrm H}_2\mathrm O{/tex}

 1

OR

Propanamine and N,N-dimethylmethanamine contain the same number of carbon atoms, even though Propanamine has higher boiling point than N,N-dimethylmethanamine. Why?

Solution:

Propanamine has intermolecular hydrogen bonding whereas N,N-dimethylmethanamine has no intermolecular hydrogen bonding.

 1

SECTION B
6.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. Aquatic species are more comfortable in cold water than warm water.
  2. At higher altitudes people suffer from anoxia resulting in inability to think.
Solution:
  1. Solubility of gases (O2) increases with decrease in temperature / Solubilty of gases ( O2) is inversely proportional to temperature / Decrease in temperature decreases KH and increases solubility of gases (O2).
  2. Due to the lower partial pressure of oxygen / Due to low concentrations of oxygen in the blood.
 2

OR

What type of azeotropic mixture will be formed by a solution of acetone and chloroform? Justify on the basis of strength of intermolecular interactions that develop in the solution.

Solution:

Maximum boiling azeotrope
Hydrogen bonding between acetone and chloroform / Stronger solute-solvent interaction / Negative deviation from Raoult’s law.

 2
7.

Explain with a graph, the variation of molar conductivity of a strong electrolyte with dilution.

Solution:


{tex}\Lambda{/tex}m increases with increase in dilution / {tex}\Lambda{/tex}m = {tex}К{/tex}V / with increase in volume {tex}\Lambda{/tex}m increases.

 2
8.

When dilute ferrous sulphate solution is added to an aqueous solution containing nitrate ion followed by careful addition of concentrated sulphuric acid along the sides of test tube, a brown ring is formed at the interface between the solution and sulphuric acid layers. Which anion is confirmed by the appearance of brown ring? What is the composition of the brown ring?

Solution:

A brown ring wall from at the junction of the two layers ,indicting the presence of nitrate ion. The brown ring formed in the confirmatory test for the nitrate ion is due to the complex called nitros ferrous sulphate with the formula [Fe(H2O)5(NO)]SO4.

 2

OR

How can you prepare Cl2 from HCl and HCl from Cl2 ? Write reactions only.

Solution:
  1. Cl2 can be prepared from HCl by Deacon's process.
    {tex}4HCl + {O_2}\xrightarrow{{CuC{l_2}}}2C{l_2} + 2{H_2}O{/tex}
  2. HCl can be prepared from Cl2 on treating it with water.
    {tex}C{l_2} + {H_2}O \to \mathop {HCl}\limits_{Hydrochloric\;acid} + \mathop {HOCl}\limits_{Hypochlorous\;acid}{/tex}
 2
9.

Use the data to answer the following and also justify giving reason:

  Cr Mn Fe Co
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{M}^{2+} / \mathrm{M}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} -0.91 -1.18 -0.44 -0.28
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{M}^{3+} / \mathrm{M}^{2+}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} -0.41 +1.57 +0.77 +1.97
  1. Which is a stronger reducing agent in aqueous medium, Cr2+ or Fe2+ and why?
  2. Which is the most stable ion in +2 oxidation and why?
Solution:
  1. Cr2+, due to lower standard reduction potential (Eo) and Higher standard oxidation potential.
  2. Mn2+, Due to highest negative standard reduction potential.
 2
10.

Define with equation:

  1. Reimer-Tiemann reaction
  2. Williamson's Synthesis
Solution:
  1. Reimer-Tiemann reaction:
  2. Williamson’s synthesis:
 2
11.

Write the names and structures of monomers in the following polymers:

  1. Nylon-6,6
  2. Buna-S
Solution:

Chapter Deleted from the Textbook.

 2
12.

Classify the following as addition and condensation polymers giving reason:

  1. Teflon
  2. PHBV
Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.
Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 2

SECTION C
13.

Chromium crystallises in bcc structure. If its edge length is 300 pm, find its density. Atomic mass of chromium is 52 u. [NA = 6.022 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 mol-1]

Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.
Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 3
14.

At 300 K, 30 g of glucose present in a litre of its solution has an osmotic pressure of 4.98 bar. If the osmotic pressure of a glucose solution is 1.52 bar at the same temperature, what would be its concentration?

Solution:

{tex}\pi{/tex} = CRT
4.98 = (30/180/1) {tex}\times{/tex} RT
4.98= 0.166 RT …(i)
1.52 = CRT …(ii)
Divide eq. (ii) by (i)
0.305 = C/0.166
C = 0.0506 mol l-1
Hence, its concentration would be 0.0506 mol l-1

 3
15.

Calculate {tex}\Delta_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{G}^{\circ}{/tex} and log Kc for the following reaction:
Cd+2(aq) + Zn(s) {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Zn2+(aq) + Cd(s)
Given: {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cd}}^{\mathrm{o}} 2+/ \mathrm{_C}_{d}{/tex} = -0.403 V
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}}^{\mathrm{O}} 2+/ \mathrm{_Z}{_n}{/tex} = -0.763 V

Solution:

{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}^o{/tex} = Ecathode - Eanode
= -0.403 - (-0.763) = 0.360 V
{tex}\Delta_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{G}^o{/tex} = {tex}-\mathrm{nFE}{ }_{\text {cell }}^o{/tex}
= -2 {tex}\times{/tex} 96500 {tex}\times{/tex} f0.360
= -69480 J mol-1 or -69.480 kJ mol-1
Hence, {tex}\Delta_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{G}^{\circ}{/tex} for the given reaction is  -69.480 kJ mol-1
log Kc = {tex}\mathrm{nE}^o_ {cell }{/tex}
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.360 / 0.059
log Kc = 12.20

 3

OR

Chromium metal is electroplated using an acidic solution containing CrO3 according to the following equation:
CrO3(aq) + 6H+ + 6e- {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Cr(s) + 3H2O
Calculate how many grams of chromium will be electroplated by 24,000 coulombs. How long will it take to electroplate 1.5 g chromium using 12.5 A current?
[Atomic mass of Cr = 52 g mol-1, 1 F = 96500 C mol-1]

Solution:

6 {tex}\times{/tex} 96500 C deposit 1 mole Cr = 52 g
24000 C will deposit 52 {tex}\times{/tex} 24000 / 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 96500 = 2.155 g
Hence, 2.155 g grams of chromium will be electroplated by 24,000 coulombs.
52 g of Cr deposited by 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 96500 C
1.5 g Cr deposited by 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 96500 {tex}\times{/tex} 1.5 / 52 = 16701.9 C
Q = I {tex}\times{/tex} t
t = Q/I = 16701.9 C / 12.5 A = 1336 s

 3
16.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. Leather gets hardened after tanning.

18.

Explain the method of preparation of sodium dichromate from chromite ore. Give the equation representing oxidation of ferrous salts by dichromate ion.

Solution:

Fusion of chromite ore (FeCr2O4) with sodium or potassium carbonate in free access of air to form sodium chromate
4 FeCr2O4 + 8 Na2CO3 + 7 O2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 8 Na2CrO4 + 2 Fe2O3 + 8 CO2
On further acidification of Sodium chromate with sulphuric acid sodium dichromate get formed.
2Na2CrO4 + 2 H+ {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Na2Cr2O7 + 2 Na+ + H2O
Cr2O72- + 14 H+ + 6 Fe2+ {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2 Cr3+ + 6 Fe3+ + 7 H2O

 3

OR

Complete the following reactions:

  1. MnO2 + KOH + O2 {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
  2. I- + {tex}\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}{/tex} + H+ {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
  3. {tex}\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}{/tex} + Sn2+ + H+ {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
Solution:
  1. 2MnO2 + 4KOH + O2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2K2MnO4 + 2H2O
  2. 10I- + {tex}2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}{/tex} + 16H+ {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2Mn2+ + 8H2O + 5 I2
  3. Cr2O72- + 14 H+ + 3 Sn2+ {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2 Cr3+ + 3 Sn4+ + 7 H2O
    Above mentioned reactions are complete reactions of given reations.

  2. [Ni(CN)4]2-

[Atomic number: Fe = 26, Ni = 28]

  1. The hybridization and magnetic character of [Fe(H2O)6]2+ are sp3d2  and paramagnetic.
  2. The hybridization and magnetic character of [Ni(CN)4]2- are dsp2 and diamagnetic

Give reasons for the following:

  2. p-dicholorobenzene has higher melting point than that of ortho or meta isomer.
  3. Thionyl chloride method is preferred for preparing alkyl chloride from alcohols.
Solution:
  1. -NO2 is an electron withdrawing group and It stabilises carbanion through resonance
  2. Due to symmetry it fits better in crystal lattice.
  3. Due to formation of gaseous byproducts.

  1. CH3CH2CH2CH2-Cl + NaI {tex}\xrightarrow{Dry \ acetone }{/tex} CH3CH2CH2CH2-l + NaCI
  2. 2-Bromo-2-methylbutane is more reactive as it gives more substituted alkene on elimination / Gives more stable product according to Saytzeff Rule.
  3. IUPAC name of given compound is 4-Bromo-4-methylpent-2- ene.

22.
  1. Give one chemical test to distinguish between the compounds of the following pairs:
    1. CH3NH2 and (CH3)2NH
    2. Aniline and Ethanamine
  2. Why aniline does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction?
Solution:
    1. CH3NH2 with Hinsberg’s Reagent (Benzene Sulphonyl Chloride) forms a precipitate which is soluble in base.
      (CH3)2NH with Hinsberg’s Reagent (Benzene Sulphonyl Chloride) forms a precipitate which is insoluble in base.
    2. Add benzene diazoniumchloride to both the compounds, aniline forms yellow dye while ethanamine does not.
  2. Due to salt formation between Aniline and anhydrous AlCl3.
    Aniline behave as lewis base and anhydrous AlCl3 behaves as lewis acid.
    Nitrogen of aniline acquires positive charge which acts as a strong deactivating group.
    These are the reasons thats why aniline does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction.
 3
23.
  1. Give any one property of glucose that cannot be explained by the open chain structure.
  2. Compare amylase with amylopectin in terms of constituting structure.
  3. Why do amino acids show amphoteric behaviour?
Solution:
  1. Glucose does not give Schiff’s test
  2. Amylose is a long unbranched chain with {tex}\alpha{/tex}-glucose units / Glycosidic linkage between C-1 & C-4.
    Amylopectin is a branched chain polymer of {tex}\alpha{/tex}-D-glucose Units / Chain is formed by C1-C4 glycosidic linkage whereas branching occurs by C1-C6 glycosidic linkage.
    Note: As per the language of question paper “Amylase” a protein / enzyme, a polymer of {tex}\alpha{/tex}-amino acid.
  3. Due to presence of both, acidic (-COOH) and basic (-NH2) groups / It reacts with both, acids and bases.

Solution:
    1. The order of reaction iis Zero order.
    2. -k
  2. {tex}\log \frac{k_2}{k_1}=\frac{E_{\mathrm{a}}}{2.303 R}\left[\frac{T_2-T_1}{T_1 T_2}\right]{/tex}
    log4 = {tex}\left(\frac{E_{\mathrm{a}}}{2.303 \times 8.314 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}}\right)\left[\frac{313-293}{313 \times 293}\right]{/tex}
    0.602 = Ea {tex}\times{/tex} 20/19.147 {tex}\times{/tex} 91709
    Ea = 52862 j mol-1 = 52.862 kJ mol-1
 5

OR
  1. Draw the plot of ln k vs 1/T for a chemical reaction. What does the intercept represent? What is the relation between slope and Ea?
  2. A first order reaction takes 30 minutes for 20% decomposition. Calculate t1/2. [log 2 = 0.3010]
Solution:
  2. k = (2.303/t) log [R]0 / [R]
    = (2.303 / 30 min) log 100/80
    = 0.0074 min-1 or 0.007 min-1
    t ½ = 0.693 /k
    = 0.693 / 0.0074 = 93.6 min or 99 min
    OR
    t1/2 = (2.303 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.3010 {tex}\times{/tex} 30) / (2.303 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.0969)
    = 93.16 min
    Hence, half life for the given reaction is 93.16 min.

    1. PH3 < AsH3 < NH3 < SbH3 < BiH3 / NH3 > PH3 < AsH3 < SbH3 < BiH3; NH3 molecules associated with intermolecular H-bonding while other hydrides are associated with Van der Waals forces which depends on size.

Solution:
  2. Compound B gives positive Fehling's test. It shows that it is an aldehyde and gives an iodoform test which shows it has a {tex}{COCH}_3{/tex} group. Compounds C is a ketone because it does not give Fehling's test but gives iodoform test which shows it also has {tex}{COCH}_3{/tex} groups.
    Hence compound A is {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\mathrm{CH}=\underset{{\mathrm{CH}}_3}{\underset\vert{\mathrm C}}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3{/tex}

    2. {tex}\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO}+3 \mathrm{I} 2+\mathrm{NaOH} \xrightarrow{\text { Hydrolysis }} \underset{\text { Iodoform }}{\mathrm{CHI}_3+3 \mathrm{HI}+\mathrm{HCOONa}}{/tex}

    2. CH3Br {tex}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{KCN} \text { (alc.) }}{/tex} CH3CN {tex}\xrightarrow[\mathrm{H}^{+} / \mathrm{OH}^{-}]{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}{/tex} CH3COOH {tex}\xrightarrow[\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}]{\mathrm{LiAlH} / \text { Ether or } \mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6}{/tex}
    1. Major product for given reaction is CH3CH2CH3

answer_key_text: Annual Exam - 2019 Code: 56/4/1 (set-1) Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate. Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions. Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period. CHEMISTRY (Theory) Time allowed:3 hours Maximum Marks:70 General Instructions : All questions are compulsory. Section A : Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each. Section B : Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each. Section C : Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each. Section D : Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed. SECTION A 1. Name the defect in the following crystal: Solution: Chapter deleted from the textbook. 1 2. When a coordination compound CrCl 3 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 6H 2 O is mixed with AgNO 3 , two moles of AgCl are precipitated per mole of the compound. What is the structural formula of the coordination compound? Solution: [Cr (H 2 O) 5 Cl]Cl 2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} H 2 O / [Cr (H 2 O) 5 Cl]Cl 2 1 OR What is the difference between a complex and a double salt? Solution: Double salt dissociates in simple ions completely when dissolved in water while Complex salt does not. 1 3. Define associated colloid with an example. Solution: Chapter deleted from the textbook. 1 4. Why is t-butyl bromide more reactive towards S N 1 reaction as compared to n-butyl bromide? Solution: Due to higher stability of 3 o / tertiary carbocation 1 5. Write the reaction involved in the Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction. Solution: {tex}\mathrm R-\overset{\overset{\mathrm O}{\vert\vert}}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{NH}}_2+{\mathrm{Br}}_2+4\mathrm{NaOH}\rightarrow R-{\mathrm{NH}}_2+{\mathrm{Na}}_2{\mathrm{CO}}_3+2\mathrm{NaBr}+2{\mathrm H}_2\mathrm O{/tex} 1 OR Propanamine and N,N-dimethylmethanamine contain the same number of carbon atoms, even though Propanamine has higher boiling point than N,N-dimethylmethanamine. Why? Solution: Propanamine has intermolecular hydrogen bonding whereas N,N-dimethylmethanamine has no intermolecular hydrogen bonding. 1 SECTION B 6. Give reasons for the following: Aquatic species are more comfortable in cold water than warm water. At higher altitudes people suffer from anoxia resulting in inability to think. Solution: Solubility of gases (O 2 ) increases with decrease in temperature / Solubilty of gases ( O 2 ) is inversely proportional to temperature / Decrease in temperature decreases K H and increases solubility of gases (O 2 ). Due to the lower partial pressure of oxygen / Due to low concentrations of oxygen in the blood. 2 OR What type of azeotropic mixture will be formed by a solution of acetone and chloroform? Justify on the basis of strength of intermolecular interactions that develop in the solution. Solution: Maximum boiling azeotrope Hydrogen bonding between acetone and chloroform / Stronger solute-solvent interaction / Negative deviation from Raoult’s law. 2 7. Explain with a graph, the variation of molar conductivity of a strong electrolyte with dilution. Solution: {tex}\Lambda{/tex} m increases with increase in dilution / {tex}\Lambda{/tex} m = {tex}К{/tex} V / with increase in volume {tex}\Lambda{/tex} m increases. 2 8. When dilute ferrous sulphate solution is added to an aqueous solution containing nitrate ion followed by careful addition of concentrated sulphuric acid along the sides of test tube, a brown ring is formed at the interface between the solution and sulphuric acid layers. Which anion is confirmed by the appearance of brown ring? What is the composition of the brown ring? Solution: A brown ring wall from at the junction of the two layers ,indicting the presence of nitrate ion . The brown ring formed in the confirmatory test for the nitrate ion is due to the complex called nitros ferrous sulphate with the formula [Fe(H 2 O) 5 (NO)]SO 4 . 2 OR How can you prepare Cl 2 from HCl and HCl from Cl 2 ? Write reactions only. Solution: Cl 2 can be prepared from HCl by Deacon's process. {tex}4HCl + {O_2}\xrightarrow{{CuC{l_2}}}2C{l_2} + 2{H_2}O{/tex} HCl can be prepared from Cl 2 on treating it with water. {tex}C{l_2} + {H_2}O \to \mathop {HCl}\limits_{Hydrochloric\;acid} + \mathop {HOCl}\limits_{Hypochlorous\;acid}{/tex} 2 9. Use the data to answer the following and also justify giving reason: Cr Mn Fe Co {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{M}^{2+} / \mathrm{M}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} -0.91 -1.18 -0.44 -0.28 {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{M}^{3+} / \mathrm{M}^{2+}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} -0.41 +1.57 +0.77 +1.97 Which is a stronger reducing agent in aqueous medium, Cr 2+ or Fe 2+ and why? Which is the most stable ion in +2 oxidation and why? Solution: Cr 2+ , due to lower standard reduction potential (E o ) and Higher standard oxidation potential. Mn 2+ , Due to highest negative standard reduction potential. 2 10. Carry out the following conversions: P-nitrotoluene to 2-bromobenzoic acid Propanoic acid to acetic acid An alkene with molecular formula C 5 H 10 on ozonolysis gives a mixture of two compounds, B and C. Compound B gives positive Fehling test and also reacts with iodine and NaOH solution. Compound C does not give Fehling solution test but forms iodoform. Identify the compounds A, B and C. Solution: Compound B gives positive Fehling's test. It shows that it is an aldehyde and gives an iodoform test which shows it has a {tex}{COCH}_3{/tex} group. Compounds C is a ketone because it does not give Fehling's test but gives iodoform test which shows it also has {tex}{COCH}_3{/tex} groups.
